text.title: devops-lb-ssl-tls Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-lb-ssl-tls as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the default Nginx ssl_ciphers or HAProxy cipher list without explicitly disabling legacy ciphers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default cipher lists historically include RC4, 3DES, and CBC-mode ciphers for backward compatibility. Attackers can force downgrade to these weak ciphers if the client supports them. Always specify an explicit allowlist.

failure-mode.description: Storing TLS private keys as unencrypted PEM files owned by the web server user with world-readable permissions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any process running as that user can read the private key and decrypt past or future sessions. Use filesystem permissions (mode 600, root ownership) and ideally inject keys from a secrets manager at runtime.
